items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell After Cavs Close Out Raptors In 7 Games: 'We Play In A Couple Days'
items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell After Cavs Close Out Raptors In 7 Games: 'We Play In A Couple Days' — The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors 114-102 in Game 7 to close out the series on Sunday night. Jarrett Allen tied a playoff career high with 22 points and 19 rebounds. Donovan Mitchell added 22 points, with James Harden finishing with 18 points. items.headline: The 'Active 30-point playoff games' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Active 30-point playoff games' quiz — How many of the active NBA players with at least 10 30-point games in the postseason can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Timberwolves' Anthony Edwards may return sooner than expected for series against Spurs
items.blurb: Timberwolves' Anthony Edwards may return sooner than expected for series against Spurs — Minnesota Timberwolves guard Anthony Edwards is itching to return to postseason action. Minnesota was initially expected to be without Edwards (left knee) for the first two games of its series against the San Antonio Spurs.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic knows who to blame for Nuggets' epic playoff collapse
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic knows who to blame for Nuggets' epic playoff collapse — The Denver Nuggets have some tough decisions to make in the offseason. Failing to take down the No. 6 seed Minnesota Timberwolves without Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu might be the biggest blemish in Nikola Jokic's postseason career so far.

items.headline: Barnes praises Raptors teammates: ‘I couldn’t be more proud’
items.blurb: Barnes praises Raptors teammates: ‘I couldn’t be more proud’ — Toronto Raptors star Scottie Barnes recaps his team’s first-round series against the Cleveland Cavaliers and breaks down what he learned about his teammates throughout their journey.

items.headline: ‘Exactly what this team needed’: Rajakovic optimistic of Raptors’ future
items.blurb: ‘Exactly what this team needed’: Rajakovic optimistic of Raptors’ future — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic speaks about Scottie Barnes’ trajectory and discusses how the team’s season was an overall success despite coming up short in Game 7 of their first-round series against the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: Jarrett Allen's Game 7 performance matches LeBron James in Cavaliers' history
items.blurb: Jarrett Allen's Game 7 performance matches LeBron James in Cavaliers' history — One key role player made history as the Cleveland Cavaliers punched their ticket to the second round with a 114-102 win over the Toronto Raptors on Sunday night. While it took a total team effort for the Cavaliers to clinch their first-round series in seven games, Jarrett Allen stepped up more than anyone else.

items.headline: How third quarter cost the Raptors in Game 7 loss
items.blurb: How third quarter cost the Raptors in Game 7 loss — The Raptors Panel breaks down how the third quarter proved to be too much to overcome in a tough Game 7 loss to the Cavaliers, and also touches on Jarrett Allen’s dominant effort in the closeout game.

items.headline: Raptors run out of road in season that exceeded expectations
items.blurb: Raptors run out of road in season that exceeded expectations — The Toronto Raptors’ season has come to an end.

items.headline: Pistons Defeat Magic 116-94 In Game 7
items.blurb: Pistons Defeat Magic 116-94 In Game 7 — The Detroit Pistons defeated the Orlando Magic 116-94 in Game 7 to complete the comeback and win the first-round series. Cade Cunningham finished the game with 32 points, with Tobias Harris adding 30 points. "No one can ever say [stuff] to me about Tobias Harris," Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff said. items.headline: Nuggets To Retain David Adelman As Head Coach, Looking To Sign Nikola Jokic To Long-Term Extension
items.blurb: Nuggets To Retain David Adelman As Head Coach, Looking To Sign Nikola Jokic To Long-Term Extension — David Adelman will return as the head coach of the Denver Nuggets, according to Shams Charania of ESPN. "He will be back. His job is safe," said Charania. The Nuggets finished with a 54-28 record this season. They were the third seed in the Western Conference before losing to the Timberwolves in th

items.headline: Luka Doncic To Remain Out For Start Of Series Versus Thunder
items.blurb: Luka Doncic To Remain Out For Start Of Series Versus Thunder — Luka Doncic will remain out for the start of the Los Angeles Lakers' series against the Oklahoma City Thunder, according to Shams Charania of ESPN. Doncic, who is recovering from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, is being evaluated on a week-to-week timeline. "The Lakers are evaluating Doncic on a week-to-week basis. He's missed about a mont

items.headline: Sabrina Ionescu To Undergo MRI On Injured Ankle
items.blurb: Sabrina Ionescu To Undergo MRI On Injured Ankle — Sabrina Ionescu is set to undergo an MRI on her injured left ankle following the New York Liberty's 79-67 win in preseason over the Connecticut Sun. "[Things are] positive right now," first-year Liberty coach Chris DeMarco said. "We're just going to do the test tomorrow and that's the only update I have." It is the same ankle that Ionescu required

items.headline: Obinna Ekezie Commits To Louisville, Will Reclassify To 2026 Class
items.blurb: Obinna Ekezie Commits To Louisville, Will Reclassify To 2026 Class — Center Obinna Ekezie committed to Louisville on Sunday and will reclassify into the class of 2026. Ekezie was ranked as a top-five prospect in the junior class. He is also ranked as the No. 1 center in the junior class, and will push to be the top-ranked center for the senior class. "I have chosen to commit to University of Louis

items.headline: Raptors’ Shead lobs it up to Murray-Boyles for vicious jam
items.blurb: Raptors’ Shead lobs it up to Murray-Boyles for vicious jam — Watch as Jamal Shead throws up an alley-oop to Collin Murray-Boyles, who throws it down over Jarrett Allen.

items.headline: Banchero wonders about Magic’s future after playoff collapse
items.blurb: Banchero wonders about Magic’s future after playoff collapse — Shortly after Banchero’s Orlando Magic lost 116-94 to the Detroit Pistons on Sunday in Game 7 of their playoff series, he was asked if they have enough talent to win in the NBA.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham's words mean even more after Pistons' thrilling Game 7 win
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham's words mean even more after Pistons' thrilling Game 7 win — Cunningham and the Pistons did what's so difficult to do in the heat of the NBA playoffs. They pulled off three consecutive wins to advance.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham, Tobias Harris make history in Pistons' Game 7 win over Magic
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham, Tobias Harris make history in Pistons' Game 7 win over Magic — The Detroit Pistons capped off their first-round series against the Orlando Magic on Sunday with a Game 7 116-94 win, advancing them to the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Celtics’ Tatum has no regrets after ‘unfortunate’ ending to season
items.blurb: Celtics’ Tatum has no regrets after ‘unfortunate’ ending to season — Jayson Tatum believes he would have been able to play if the Boston Celtics advanced to the second round of the NBA playoffs.

items.headline: Brandon Ingram Out For Game 7 Versus Cavs
items.blurb: Brandon Ingram Out For Game 7 Versus Cavs — The Toronto Raptors ruled Brandon Ingram out for Sunday's Game 7 against the Cleveland Cavaliers due to right heel inflammation. Ingram also missed Game 6 with the injury. Ingram is averaging 12.0 points and 3.2 rebounds in the playoffs.

items.headline: Timberwolves’ Edwards cleared for on-court activity, questionable vs. Spurs
items.blurb: Timberwolves’ Edwards cleared for on-court activity, questionable vs. Spurs — The Minnesota Timberwolves could welcome back their leading scorer sooner than originally expected.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham's monster performance leads Pistons to second round
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham's monster performance leads Pistons to second round — It has been a long time coming, but the Detroit Pistons are finally back through to the second round of the NBA playoffs.

items.headline: Pistons’ Jenkins elevates over Magic’s Wagner to hit buzzer-beater three
items.blurb: Pistons’ Jenkins elevates over Magic’s Wagner to hit buzzer-beater three — Watch as Detroit Pistons’ Daniss Jenkins elevates over Orlando Magic’s big man Moritz Wagner to hit a buzzer-beater three-point bucket at the end of the third quarter.

items.headline: Celtics' Jayson Tatum clarifies severity of left knee injury following Game 7 loss to 76ers
items.blurb: Celtics' Jayson Tatum clarifies severity of left knee injury following Game 7 loss to 76ers — Tatum missed Game 7, which culminated in the Celtics suffering a rare playoff collapse.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards Upgraded To Questionable For Game 1 Versus Spurs
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Upgraded To Questionable For Game 1 Versus Spurs — Minnesota Timberwolves guard Anthony Edwards has been upgraded to questionable for Game 1 against the San Antonio Spurs with a bone bruise in his left knee. It was reported earlier on Sunday that the Wolves have "growing optimism" that Edwards could return to the lineup by Game 3 or 4 against the Spurs, sources told Shams Charania

items.headline: Blazers Expect Damian Lillard To Return Next Season; Will Receive Trade Interest In Jrue Holiday
items.blurb: Blazers Expect Damian Lillard To Return Next Season; Will Receive Trade Interest In Jrue Holiday — The Portland Trail Blazers expect Damian Lillard to return to the lineup next season after the guard suffered a torn Achilles in April 2025. The Blazers could potentially have a starting backcourt of Lillard and Jrue Holiday, though the team also expects Holiday to receive trade interest this offseas

items.headline: Anthony Edwards has target set for his injury return
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards has target set for his injury return — He has not played since suffering the injury in Game 4 of the Timberwolves' first-round series against the Denver Nuggets.

items.headline: Matt Lloyd Takes Lead To Be New Bulls' Head Of Basketball Operations; Would Hire Micah Nori As Coach
items.blurb: Matt Lloyd Takes Lead To Be New Bulls' Head Of Basketball Operations; Would Hire Micah Nori As Coach — Minnesota Timberwolves general manager Matt Lloyd has reportedly taken the lead to become the new head of basketball operations for the Chicago Bulls, sources told Joe Cowley of the Chicago Sun-Times. Lloyd could be hired for the job over the next few days. Lloyd previously worked for the Bulls,

items.blurb: Jaylen Brown Says This Season Was 'One Of My Most Fun Years Playing Basketball' — Jaylen Brown reflected on the Boston Celtics' season following their Game 7 loss to the Philadelphia 76ers in the first round. items.headline: Celtics' Jaylen Brown calls out 76ers' Joel Embiid for 'flopping around' following stunning first-round playoff collapse
items.blurb: Celtics' Jaylen Brown calls out 76ers' Joel Embiid for 'flopping around' following stunning first-round playoff collapse — Following a 109-100 loss in Game 7 at TD Garden in Boston on Saturday night, Brown called out the referees for giving Embiid favorable calls.

items.headline: Joel Embiid calls out Sixers fans with one simple request vs. the Knicks
items.blurb: Joel Embiid calls out Sixers fans with one simple request vs. the Knicks — Against all odds, the Philadelphia 76ers overcame a 3-1 series deficit in the first round to knock out the No. 2-seeded Boston Celtics. Now, Nick Nurse's team will face the New York Knicks in the second round and another edition of this rivalry between towns.

items.headline: Joel Embiid has a special message for Payton Pritchard after Game 7
items.blurb: Joel Embiid has a special message for Payton Pritchard after Game 7 — Embiid clearly noticed comments Pritchard made prior to Embiid's return to action.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell: Posts 22 points in Game 7 win
items.blurb: Donovan Mitchell: Posts 22 points in Game 7 win — Mitchell produced 22 points (9-20 FG, 2-9 3Pt, 2-2 FT), three rebounds and one assist across 35 minutes during Sunday's 114-102 win over the Raptors in Game 7 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jarrett Allen: Massive double-double in Game 7 win
items.blurb: Jarrett Allen: Massive double-double in Game 7 win — Allen amassed 22 points (7-11 FG, 8-14 FT), 19 rebounds, three blocks, two assists and two steals across 33 minutes in Sunday's 114-102 Game 7 win over the Raptors in the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Scottie Barnes: Paces Toronto in season-ending loss
items.blurb: Scottie Barnes: Paces Toronto in season-ending loss — Barnes posted 24 points (8-14 FG, 1-1 3Pt, 7-7 FT), nine rebounds, six assists and one block across 37 minutes in Sunday's 114-102 Game 7 loss to the Cavaliers in the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham: Leads Detroit to next round
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham: Leads Detroit to next round — Cunningham finished with 32 points (10-18 FG, 4-6 3Pt, 8-10 FT), one rebound, 12 assists and two blocks across 39 minutes in Sunday's 116-94 win over Orlando in Game 7 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Paolo Banchero: Erupts for 38 points in Game 7 loss
items.blurb: Paolo Banchero: Erupts for 38 points in Game 7 loss — Banchero supplied 38 points (14-25 FG, 4-7 3Pt, 6-8 FT), nine rebounds, six assists and one block across 42 minutes in Sunday's 116-94 Game 7 loss to the Pistons in the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Hawks Unlikely To Make Big Trade This Offseason, Focused On Internal Improvements
items.blurb: Hawks Unlikely To Make Big Trade This Offseason, Focused On Internal Improvements — The Atlanta Hawks are not looking to swing a big trade this offseason, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. items.headline: Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey Lead 76ers Past Celtics In Game 7 To Complete Historic 3-1 Comeback
items.blurb: Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey Lead 76ers Past Celtics In Game 7 To Complete Historic 3-1 Comeback — Joel Embiid scored 34 points with 12 rebounds and six assists Saturday as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 109-100 in Game 7, completing the first comeback from a 3-1 series deficit in franchise history. The Sixers advance to face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference semifinal

items.blurb: Blazers To Be 'Prime Trade Suitor' For Giannis Antetokounmpo, May Pursue Anthony Davis As Alternative — The Portland Trail Blazers are set to emerge as a "prime trade suitor" for Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. The Blazers discussed deals for Antetokounmpo with the Bucks before the trade deadline. The team is looking to build a trade package

items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Tells Teammates He Plans To Return During Series Against Spurs — Anthony Edwards has informed his Minnesota Timberwolves teammates he intends to return at some point during their second-round series against the San Antonio Spurs, team sources told The Athletic. items.blurb: Five incredible 76ers-Celtics Game 7 stats: Joe Mazzulla's lineup gamble was massive misfire — The Philadelphia 76ers and Boston Celtics played in the first of what we can only hope is three Eastern Conference Game 7 thrillers on Saturday, a 109-100 76ers win.
